👈 Select a department from the sidebar

❓ How to Use — Departmental Report V2.2

1. Sidebar — Department List

The left sidebar lists all departments. Click a department name to open its Role Matrix. The active department is highlighted in blue.

  • ✏️ Rename — click to edit the department name inline. Press Enter or click away to save.
  • ▲ / ▼ Move — reorder the department up or down in the sidebar.
  • ✕ Delete — permanently removes the department and all its data. You will be asked to confirm.

Use the tabs at the top of the sidebar to switch between ✏️ Edit Role Matrix and 📋 Edit Competencies.

2. Edit Role Matrix Tab

Displays every role in the selected department as a table. Each row = one person / one role.

  • FC Code — functional competency code (e.g. QA-01).
  • Role — job title / role name.
  • Name — employee's name.
  • ID No. — employee ID.
  • Req / Cur / Gap — required level, current level, and gap (Cur − Req) for each competency. Levels 1–6; 0 = not assessed.

Click any cell to edit it directly. Gap cells are calculated automatically and are read-only.

3. Toolbar Buttons (Role Matrix)

  • 📂 Load Excel File — import data from an Excel (.xlsx) file into the currently selected department. See section 4 for details.
  • ➕ Add Role — appends a new blank row to the current department's matrix.
  • 👤 Add Names — bulk-import employee names and IDs from a structured list.
  • 📋 Paste Req — paste required-level values from clipboard into the Req column.
  • 🗑️ Clear All — clears all data in the current department's matrix (keeps row structure).

4. 📂 Load Excel File

Imports role data from an Excel (.xlsx) file directly into the currently selected department. Select the department first, then click 📂 Load Excel File.

Expected Excel layout:

  • Rows 1–2 are header rows (they will be skipped). For columns A–D these two rows are merged; from column E onwards they split into two sub-header rows.
  • Column A — FC Code  |  Column B — Role  |  Column C — Name  |  Column D — ID No.
  • From Column E onwards — each competency occupies 3 columns: Req, Cur, Gap. The competency name appears in the Req (first) column header only.
  • Data starts at row 3. One row per person.

Loading replaces all existing rows in the selected department. Competency names are read dynamically from the Excel header — they do not need to match the existing list.

5. Edit Competencies Tab

Manage the list of competencies for the selected department. Add, rename, or remove competencies. Changes are reflected immediately in the Role Matrix columns.

6. 💾 Save

Downloads the current working file (this HTML page with all data embedded) so you can continue editing later. Open the downloaded file in your browser to resume.

7. 📄 Export Dept Report

Generates a clean, read-only HTML report for the currently selected department. The exported file can be sent to the Role Matrix Aggregator for cross-department analysis. Only the active department is exported.

8. Tips

  • All changes are held in memory until you click 💾 Save — save often.
  • If you accidentally delete data, close the tab without saving and reopen your last saved file.
  • Gap column highlights in red when the current level is below required.
  • This is a blank template (V2.2) — use 📂 Load Excel File to populate it quickly from existing data.
